events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to identify the component or condition, and name visible defect cues. The scenario: Production welding, field condition: spatter/cleanliness reject. The work is rough-complete. Reference: AWS D1.1/D1.3, shop WPS.

We need to output JSON with findings, actions, rationale, etc. The decision should likely be "fail" because spatter/cleanliness reject indicates a defect. Risk: spatter can affect weld quality, cleanliness issues might lead to corrosion or contamination. Likely medium risk. Work stage: rough-complete as given. Component conditions: installed-defective? Or non-compliant? Spatter is a defect, so installed-defective seems appropriate. Percent complete? Rough-complete might be around 80-90% but not closed out. Let's say 85%. Value: maybe not applicable, set 0. Sound source: none. Confidence high. Event time, rate: not applicable. Order: not specified, maybe empty. Workable: what activities can still start? Since it's defective, maybe rework needed, so workable could be empty or list rework. Findings: "spatter and cleanliness reject observed on weld". Actions: "stop work, clean spatter, re-inspect, review WPS". Rationale: "Excessive spatter violates AWS D1.1/D1.3 cleanliness requirements; can lead to corrosion, hydrogen embrittlement, or coating failure." References: "AWS D1.1, shop WPS".

Let's construct the JSON. We must ensure only one JSON object.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "medium",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Excessive spatter and surface contamination observed on production weld.",
    "Weld cleanliness does not meet acceptance criteria per AWS D1.1/D1.3."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop welding activity immediately.",
    "Remove spatter and clean weld surface.",
    "Re-inspect for compliance before proceeding.",
    "Review shop WPS for parameters and shielding gas settings to prevent recurrence."
  ],
  "rationale": "Spatter and cleanliness issues can lead to reduced weld integrity, coating failure, or corrosion. They violate visual acceptance criteria in AWS D1.1/D1.3 and the shop WPS.",
  "references": [
    "AWS D1.1 Structural Welding Code – Steel, Section 6.9 Visual Inspection Acceptance Criteria",
    "AWS D1.3 Structural Welding Code – Sheet Steel, Section 7.9",
    "shop WPS cleanliness requirements"
  ]
}
